title: MongoDB GridFS - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Storing and retrieving large files in MongoDB

overview.definition: MongoDB specification for storing files larger than 16MB
overview.concept: Split large files into chunks and store in collections
purpose: Store files larger than 16MB BSON document limit
Keep files with database for consistency
overview.how_it_works: Divides file into chunks (255KB default), stores metadata separately
alternatives: External storage (S3, GCS) with metadata in MongoDB

concepts.description: File info stored in fs.files collection
File data stored in fs.chunks collection

beginner_examples.title: Upload image file
Download file by filename

intermediate_examples.title: Upload with metadata

practices.why: S3/GCS better for large-scale file storage
